title: asteriscus spinosus subsp. microcephalus
intro: Asteriscus spinosus subsp. microcephalus, also known as Dwarf Marsh Cudweed, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small size and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Asteriscus spinosus subsp. microcephalus include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antidiabetic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are commonly made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
